detail.evidence: TestRandomUUID generates 31 UUIDs via New() and checks version==4 and variant==RFC4122. However, the test does NOT verify that the UUIDs actually contain 122 random bits. The code sets uuid[6] = (uuid[6] & 0x0f) | 0x40 (version 4) and uuid[8] = (uuid[8] & 0x3f) | 0x80 (variant RFC4122), but the test never inspects the actual bit values to confirm the 122 random bits are present—it only checks the version and variant fields after they are already masked and set by the code. The test passes as long as Version() and Variant() return the expected values, which they will regardless of the actual entropy, making this test vacuous with respect to the "122 random bits" requirement.

TestRandomUUID calls uuid.Version() and uuid.Variant() on UUIDs created by New(), and asserts v == 4 and variant == RFC4122. However, the test never verifies that the 122 random bits are actually random or that they are not deterministic. The Version and Variant checks pass by virtue of the fixed bit manipulations in NewRandomFromReader (lines uuid[6] = (uuid[6] & 0x0f) | 0x40 and uuid[8] = (uuid[8] & 0x3f) | 0x80), which set those bits regardless of whether the underlying 122 bits came from a proper random source. The test does not validate the randomness property stated in the goal.

TestRandomUUID verifies that New() generates 31 UUIDs with version 4 and RFC4122 variant. However, the test does not verify the core requirement: that the UUID contains 122 random bits. The code in NewRandomFromReader and newRandomFromPool sets version and variant bits (lines uuid[6] and uuid[8]), which overwrites 6 bits of the supposed 122 random bits, reducing actual randomness. The test never validates the randomness of the 122 bits or checks that only the specified 6 bits were modified from the random source.

TestNew() calls New() 31 times and checks each has version 4 and RFC4122 variant. However, the test first parses the UUID string representation via Parse(s.String()), meaning it is not directly testing the binary output of New() but rather testing that Parse() correctly identifies version and variant from the string. The check `if v := uuid.Version(); v != 4` is actually verifying Parse's extraction logic, not New's generation logic. The test does not directly validate the bit-level structure (122 random bits, version nibble = 0x4, variant bits = 0b10) that New() produces.
